description: Can the agent prove functions when no structurally similar proof exists in the same file? Tests the genuine worst case: functions where the agent must rely entirely on imports + cross-file search, with no in-file proof template.

methodology.target_selection: 5 functions chosen for: (1) non-trivial proof body (>10 LOC GT), (2) only proof of its pattern type in its file or file with few proofs, (3) spanning 4+ layers
methodology.hole_creation: Each function's proof body replaced with admit() on a dedicated git branch

isolation.worktree_per_function: git worktree at /tmp/exp16-SN, one per candidate
agent_rules: DO NOT read files outside own worktree
isolation.agent_prompt: Sterile — contains ONLY file path, function name, verification command, and rules. ZERO strategy/pattern/LOC/difficulty hints.
isolation.orchestrator_GT_exposure: Orchestrator read all GT during candidate selection (unavoidable). Agent prompts contain no GT-derived content.

methodology.per_function_protocol: Read contract → search codebase for lemmas → generate proof → verify → fix up to 5 cycles → report
methodology.agents_used: Separate Claude Opus agent per function, each with fresh context

contamination_audit.method: grep agent transcripts for cross-worktree file access patterns
contamination_audit.S1: CLEAN — ls discovered other worktrees but did NOT read GT from them
contamination_audit.S3_original: CONTAMINATED — read reduce_lemmas.rs from S2 and S4 worktrees (full GT visible). Result invalidated.
contamination_audit.S3_redo: CLEAN — other worktrees removed before re-run. Zero cross-worktree access confirmed.
contamination_audit.root_cause_of_S3_contamination: Shared /tmp/ namespace let agent traverse to /tmp/exp16-S2/ and read unholed files. Fixed by removing other worktrees before S3 redo.

by_function.notes: Decomposed into 4 helper lemmas + main body. 2.3x GT LOC but correct. Used product_of_squares, curve equation, cancel_common_factor, axiom_edwards_add_complete.
Hardest by cycles. Case split on vr==0 vs vr>0, fundamental_div_mod + nonlinear_arith to pin quotient to 1. More concise than GT (82 vs 103 LOC).

S3 redo (original was contaminated via cross-worktree GT access). Clean run took 4 cycles / 140 LOC vs contaminated run's 1 cycle / 114 LOC. Strategy: bound each reduced limb, multiply by positional weight, sum, show total < 2*p().
by_function.contamination_note: Original S3 run INVALIDATED. Agent read GT from /tmp/exp16-S2/ and /tmp/exp16-S4/ worktrees. Redo run with other worktrees removed is the valid result.

by_function.notes: Found existing lemma_rr_equals_spec() with identical postcondition. 1 LOC delegation. Cross-file search completely solved this.

Telescoping carry cancellation. Defined weighted sum W, showed it equals two expressions (intermediate + carry_sum from stages, carry4 + t_high + nl_high + carry_sum from definitions), carry_sum cancels. Key lemmas: pow2_adds, mul_is_distributive, mul_is_associative.

comparison_to_exp17.notes: Sibling-less functions take ~2.6x more cycles than exp17's sibling-available functions. But all still pass within the 5-cycle budget. The extra cycles are spent on codebase exploration and iterative fixing, not fundamental inability.

contamination_lessons.issue: Parallel worktrees in shared /tmp/ namespace allow cross-worktree GT leakage
contamination_lessons.detection: grep agent transcripts for paths matching /tmp/exp16-S[^N]/ where N is the agent's own ID
contamination_lessons.evidence_of_impact: Contaminated S3 passed in 1 cycle / 114 LOC. Clean S3 redo took 4 cycles / 140 LOC. The difference (3 extra cycles, 26 more LOC) is the measured cost of not having GT.
contamination_lessons.mitigation: Either (a) run agents sequentially with only one worktree at a time, or (b) use filesystem-level isolation (containers, mount namespaces), or (c) add explicit path restriction rule AND remove other worktrees

pipeline_implications: Cross-file search fully compensates for missing in-file siblings. All 5 functions proved, including a 217-LOC algebraic chain and a 195-LOC telescoping proof. The pipeline does NOT need to ensure every function has an in-file sibling before generation. However, sibling-less functions cost ~3x more compile-fix cycles, so the pipeline should prioritize sibling-rich files for throughput.
